title: 2010_influence_of_dump_gap_on_external_combustor_aerodynamics_at_high_fuel_flow_
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 2]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided text and image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**
*   phi = Vz/U
*   Non-dimentional speed (ND/(yRT))
*   mass flow (m (RT)/AP)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 1 Optimum dump gap [3-5]**
    *   Headers: Flame tube depth (W/hx3), Dump gap (d/hx4)
    *   Data:
        *   3.5, 0.6
        *   4.8, 0.7-0.8
        *   5.5, 0.8-1.0
        *   Lean module combustor >6.0, >1.0?

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 1 Geometrical notation:**
    *   Components and Labels:
        *   X
        *   X2
        *   X3
        *   X4
        *   X50
        *   X5i
        *   hx2
        *   hx3
        *   hx4
        *   W
        *   d
        *   r
        *   L

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Dump gap: axial distance (d) from prediffuser exit plane to flame tube
*   Dump gap ratio: d/hx4
*   Flow coefficient (phi=Vz/U): 0.403
*   Mass flow: 4 kg s-1
*   Axial velocity: 40 m s-1
*   Mach number: 0.12
*   Reynolds number: 1.6 x 105
*   OGV Vanes: 160
*   Prediffuser area ratio (IOGV): 1.8
*   L/hin: 2.23
*   Cowl flows: 50-70% of compressor efflux
*   Dump gap ratios: 0.8, 1.2, 1.6

title: 2010_influence_of_dump_gap_on_external_combustor_aerodynamics_at_high_fuel_flow_
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's the extracted technical information from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   m = integral(pUdA) = pUA   (Equation 1)
*   p_bar = (1/m) * integral(pdm) = (1/pUA) * integral(p pUdA)  (Equation 2)
*   lambda_1-2 = (p_bar1 - p_bar2) / (p_bar1 - p1), Cp1-2 = (p2 - p1) / (p_bar1 - p1)   (Equation 3)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Components:** Inlet Louvres, Exit Louvres, Inlet Plenum, Inlet Flare and Honeycomb, Air Flow, Fan Room, Centrifugal Fan, Test Cell, Working Section, Prop Shaft, Test Cell Floor Vertical Support, Plinth, Sub-Floor Plenum, Motor

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Repeatability of mass-weighted total pressures: ±1 mm H2O (which is less than ±0.5% of the dynamic pressure at OGV exit)
*   Repeatability of total pressure loss and static pressure rise coefficients: ±0.005
*   Mass flow balance on area traverse planes was within ±1% of the rig mass flow
*   Five-hole probe area traverses locations: rig inlet (X1), rotor exit (X2), OGV exit (X3), prediffuser exit (X4), within the inner (X5i) and outer (X5o) feed annuli
*   Traverse area: 2 OGV passages at stations close to the compressor (e.g., X2 and X3) but as whole combustor sector information was required further downstream (e.g., at prediffuser exit and within each feed annuli), the traverse area was typically increased to eight OGV passages (one combustor sector).
*   ICAO standard day conditions used in the data reduction

**2. Graphs/Charts:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 3 Measurement Planes:**

    *   Bellmouth Intake
    *   IGV
    *   Rotor
    *   OGV Pre-Diffuser
    *   Generic Combustor Injector (XI)
    *   Throttle
    *   Main Throttle
    *   Five-Hole Probe Measurement Stations
        *   Inlet (X1)
        *   Rotor Exit (X2)
        *   OGV Exit (X3)
        *   Pre-Diffuser Exit (X4)
    *   Annuli
        *   Outer (X5o)
        *   Inner (X5i)
*   **Fig. 4 Combustor Details:**

    *   Diameter of "Injector"
        *   Outer
        *   Injector
    *   Dump Gap
        *   Inner

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Cowl Flow: 30%, 50%, 70%
*   Dump gap ratios: 0.8, 1.2, 1.6
*   Cowl set at 70% of the compressor efflux
*   Velocity dropping to less than 10% and 40% of the bulk mean
*   Location nearest to the wall of above 40% is desirable
